text.title: Axum Handler Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for writing type-safe, thin Axum handlers with unified error handling and validated DTOs.

rule.statement: Keep handlers thin: parse input → call Service → map result. No DB queries or business logic inside handler bodies.
rule.rationale: Thin handlers are independently testable without an HTTP runtime and keep the service layer reusable.

rule.statement: Define one `AppError` enum with `thiserror`; implement `IntoResponse` once; never let handlers return `anyhow::Error` to callers.
rule.rationale: A single error type prevents accidental 500s from unhandled error variants and centralizes HTTP status mapping.

rule.statement: Use `Result<(StatusCode, Json<T>), AppError>` when success status varies (201 vs 200); use `Result<Json<T>, AppError>` when status is always 200.
rule.rationale: Explicit status in the return type documents intent and avoids implicit 200 for resource-creation endpoints.

rule.statement: Order extractors: `State` first, then path/query, then `Json` body. Axum resolves extractors in declaration order; wrong order causes opaque trait errors.
rule.rationale: Extractor ordering is a common source of "the trait Handler is not satisfied" errors that LLMs struggle to diagnose.

rule.statement: Use `Arc<sqlx::PgPool>` (it is `Clone`) inside `AppState`; never wrap in extra `Mutex`.
rule.rationale: `sqlx::PgPool` is already connection-pool-safe; adding `Mutex` serializes all DB access unnecessarily.

rule.statement: `AppState` must satisfy `Clone + Send + Sync + 'static`. Use `Arc<T>` for non-Clone resources; never use `Rc` or `RefCell`.
rule.rationale: Axum's `with_state` requires these bounds; violating them produces compiler errors that are hard to diagnose.

rule.statement: Validate input DTOs with the `validator` crate; call `.validate()?` as the first statement in the handler body; implement `From<ValidationErrors> for AppError` to return 422.
rule.rationale: Without the `From` impl, validator errors produce 500s instead of the correct 422 Unprocessable Entity.

rule.statement: Bound JSON body size with `RequestBodyLimitLayer`; the default is unbounded and an OOM vector.
rule.rationale: A single unbounded request body can exhaust server memory with no authentication required.

rule.statement: Instrument handlers and services with `#[tracing::instrument]`; use `tracing` throughout, never `println!` or `dbg!` in committed code.
rule.rationale: Structured tracing integrates with OpenTelemetry and log aggregators; print statements disappear in production.

rule.statement: Write integration tests using `tower::ServiceExt::oneshot` against the real `Router`; test both happy path and error cases (at minimum: 200/201 and 422/404).
rule.rationale: Unit-testing handlers in isolation misses extractor resolution, middleware ordering, and state injection bugs.

antipattern.description: Handler returns `anyhow::Error` directly to the client.
antipattern.reason: Leaks internal error messages and panics on unhandled variants; every unhandled arm becomes a 500.
Any `None` or `Err` panics the task and returns a 500; use `?` with `AppError` instead.
